overrides.override_summary: Disclosure re: CA Fast Food Act (AB 1228). Compliance is solely franchisee's responsibility. McDonald's provides no training, assistance, or fees related to the Act.
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees face higher minimum wage and employment standards under the Fast Food Act; McDonald's does not share this cost.

overrides.override_summary: No acknowledgement or questionnaire waives claims under state franchise law or disclaims reliance on franchisor statements.
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects Hawaii franchisees' right to bring fraud-in-the-inducement claims despite Franchise Agreement acknowledgments.

overrides.override_summary: Release required for renewal/transfer will not apply to liability under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees cannot be required to waive franchise law protections as condition of renewal or transfer.

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon insolvency might not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law. McDonald's will enforce to extent enforceable.
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits franchisor's termination power in bankruptcy situations for Maryland franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law claims in Maryland, subject to arbitration obligations.
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees can litigate franchise law claims locally rather than in Illinois.

overrides.override_summary: Maryland law applies to claims arising under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Illinois choice-of-law for MD franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: Claims under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law must be brought within 3 years after grant of franchise.
overrides.why_it_matters: Establishes specific deadline for MD franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: 90 days' notice of termination (with 60 days to cure). 180 days' notice for non-renewal. Per MN Stat. 80C.14.
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly extends notice and cure periods for Minnesota franchisees beyond the Franchise Agreement's terms.

overrides.override_summary: Consent to transfer will not be unreasonably withheld.
overrides.why_it_matters: Provides Minnesota franchisees stronger transfer protections than the base agreement.

overrides.override_summary: Nothing in Franchise Agreement or FDD shall abrogate or reduce any MN Chapter 80C rights.
overrides.why_it_matters: Broad protection preserving all Minnesota franchise law rights.

overrides.override_summary: McDonald's protects franchisees against claims of infringement or unfair competition from authorized use of marks.
overrides.why_it_matters: Adds affirmative trademark protection obligation not in base agreement.

overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete covenants generally considered unenforceable in North Dakota. McDonald's will seek to enforce to extent allowed.
overrides.why_it_matters: North Dakota franchisees may not be bound by the 18-month/10-mile noncompete.

overrides.override_summary: North Dakota law governs claims arising under North Dakota Franchise Investment Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Illinois choice-of-law for ND franchise claims.

overrides.override_summary: Washington Franchise Investment Protection Act prevails over inconsistent FDD/agreement provisions.
overrides.why_it_matters: Broad protection for Washington franchisees — state law trumps contract terms.

overrides.override_summary: Release or waiver cannot include rights under the Act except in negotiated settlement with independent counsel.
overrides.why_it_matters: Strong waiver protection for Washington franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: Assurance of Discontinuance (AOD) with WA Attorney General: McDonald's agreed to remove no-poach employee restrictions and not enforce existing ones in Washington.
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington franchisees and their employees are free from no-poach restrictions that may still apply in other states.
